U_VAR_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a new variable with a chosen type and initial value. A variable is like a named box that stores data
U_VAR_ASSIGN_TOOLTIP: Change the value of an existing variable
U_VAR_REF_TOOLTIP: Use the current value stored in this variable
U_NUMBER_TOOLTIP: Enter a number for use in calculations or comparisons
U_STRING_TOOLTIP: Enter text content; quotation marks are added automatically
U_ARITHMETIC_TOOLTIP: Perform addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, or remainder on two numbers
U_COMPARE_TOOLTIP: Compare two values for size or equality; the result is true or false
U_LOGIC_TOOLTIP: Combine two conditions: 'and' requires both to be true; 'or' requires at least one to be true
U_LOGIC_NOT_TOOLTIP: Invert a condition: true becomes false, false becomes true
U_NEGATE_TOOLTIP: Negate a number: flip its sign (positive becomes negative, negative becomes positive)
U_IF_TOOLTIP: If the condition is true, execute the blocks inside
U_IF_ELSE_TOOLTIP: If the condition is true, execute the 'then' part; otherwise execute the 'else' part
U_COUNT_LOOP_TOOLTIP: Repeat execution: the variable counts from the start value to the end value, incrementing by 1 each time
U_WHILE_LOOP_TOOLTIP: Keep executing while the condition is true
U_BREAK_TOOLTIP: Immediately exit the loop and stop repeating
U_CONTINUE_TOOLTIP: Skip the rest of this iteration and proceed to the next one
U_FUNC_DEF_TOOLTIP: Define a function (a reusable block of code). Choose void (no return) if no result is needed
U_FUNC_CALL_TOOLTIP: Execute the specified function, optionally passing arguments
U_RETURN_TOOLTIP: End the function and send the result back to the caller
U_PRINT_TOOLTIP: Print one or more values to the screen
U_ENDL_TOOLTIP: Move the output to the next line
U_INPUT_TOOLTIP: Read a value from the keyboard into a variable
U_ARRAY_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a fixed-size array (a row of consecutive slots), indexed starting from 0
U_ARRAY_ACCESS_TOOLTIP: Get or set the value at a specific position in the array, indexed starting from 0
U_ARRAY_ASSIGN_TOOLTIP: Store a value into a specific position in the array
C_CHAR_LITERAL_TOOLTIP: Enter a single character, enclosed in single quotes
C_INCREMENT_TOOLTIP: Increase or decrease the variable's value by 1
C_COMPOUND_ASSIGN_TOOLTIP: Add to, subtract from, multiply by, divide by, or take remainder of the variable's value and store it back
C_SWITCH_TOOLTIP: Choose which code to execute based on a value, like a shortcut for multiple 'if' checks
C_CASE_TOOLTIP: When the value above equals this value, execute this code
C_DEFAULT_TOOLTIP: When none of the case values match, execute this code. Like 'none of the above' in a multiple-choice question
C_TERNARY_TOOLTIP: Choose one of two values based on a condition. A compact version of 'if...else' that directly returns a result
C_CAST_TOOLTIP: Force a value to become another type. For example, cast to double before dividing integers to get a precise result
C_BITWISE_NOT_TOOLTIP: Flip every bit: 0 becomes 1 and 1 becomes 0. Commonly used in bitmask operations
C_POINTER_ASSIGN_TOOLTIP: Modify the value at the address the pointer holds, like going to the address on a note and changing what's there
C_FOR_LOOP_TOOLTIP: A custom loop: set the initial value, check the condition each time, execute if true, then update
C_DO_WHILE_TOOLTIP: Execute the code once first, then check the condition and repeat if true
C_PRINTF_TOOLTIP: Print output using a format string. Common formats: %d integer, %f decimal, %s text, \n newline
C_SCANF_TOOLTIP: Read input using a format string. Variables need & (address-of operator), e.g. &x
C_POINTER_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a pointer variable. A pointer stores the address of another variable, like a note with an address written on it
C_POINTER_DEREF_TOOLTIP: Retrieve the value stored at the address the pointer holds
C_ADDRESS_OF_TOOLTIP: Get the memory address of a variable, which can be stored in a pointer
C_MALLOC_TOOLTIP: Request a block of memory from the system; remember to free it when done
C_FREE_TOOLTIP: Return memory previously allocated with malloc back to the system
C_STRUCT_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Define a struct that groups related variables together, like a form with multiple fields
C_STRUCT_MEMBER_ACCESS_TOOLTIP: Get or set the value of a specific field in a struct
C_STRUCT_POINTER_ACCESS_TOOLTIP: Get or set the value of a struct field through a pointer
C_STRLEN_TOOLTIP: Count the number of characters in a string (excluding the null terminator)
C_STRCMP_TOOLTIP: Compare two strings. Returns 0 if they are equal, other values if different
C_STRCPY_TOOLTIP: Copy a string to another location
C_MATH_POW_TOOLTIP: Calculate base raised to the power of exponent (returns double)
C_MATH_UNARY_TOOLTIP: Single-argument math function (requires #include <cmath>). Trig functions use radians.
C_MATH_BINARY_TOOLTIP: Two-argument math function (requires #include <cmath>). Note: atan2 takes (y, x).
CPP_VECTOR_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a vector (a resizable array) that can grow and shrink as elements are added or removed
CPP_VECTOR_PUSH_BACK_TOOLTIP: Add a new element to the end of the vector
CPP_VECTOR_SIZE_TOOLTIP: Get the number of elements in the vector
CPP_MAP_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a map that looks up a value by a key, like a dictionary
CPP_STRING_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a string variable to store and manipulate text
CPP_SORT_TOOLTIP: Sort the elements in the specified range in ascending order
CPP_CLASS_DEF_TOOLTIP: Define a class that bundles data and operations together. Public members are accessible outside; private members are internal only
CPP_NEW_TOOLTIP: Create a new object and allocate memory for it
CPP_DELETE_TOOLTIP: Delete an object created with new and free the memory
CPP_TEMPLATE_FUNCTION_TOOLTIP: Create a template function that can handle different types without writing multiple versions
CPP_STACK_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a stack, like stacking plates: the last one placed is the first one removed (LIFO)
CPP_QUEUE_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a queue, like standing in line: the first one in is the first one processed (FIFO)
CPP_SET_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Create a set where elements are unique and automatically sorted
CPP_METHOD_CALL_TOOLTIP: Call a method on an object, optionally passing arguments
CPP_METHOD_CALL_EXPR_TOOLTIP: Call a method on an object and get the result
C_RAW_CODE_TOOLTIP: Enter code directly without block conversion. Use for syntax that blocks cannot express
C_RAW_EXPRESSION_TOOLTIP: Enter an expression directly that can connect to other blocks
C_INCLUDE_TOOLTIP: Include a library so the program can use the features it provides
C_INCLUDE_LOCAL_TOOLTIP: Include a local header file you wrote
C_DEFINE_TOOLTIP: Define a constant name; wherever this name appears in the code, it will be replaced with the given value
C_IFDEF_TOOLTIP: If this name has been defined, execute the code inside
C_IFNDEF_TOOLTIP: If this name has not been defined, execute the code inside
C_COMMENT_LINE_TOOLTIP: Write a comment note; it does not affect program execution
C_USING_NAMESPACE_TOOLTIP: Allow direct use of features in this namespace without a prefix. For example, use cout instead of std::cout
C_CONST_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Declare a constant variable whose value cannot be changed after initialization
C_CONSTEXPR_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Declare a compile-time constant whose value is determined during compilation
C_AUTO_DECLARE_TOOLTIP: Declare a variable whose type is automatically deduced from the initial value
C_TYPEDEF_TOOLTIP: Create a type alias: give an existing type a new name
C_USING_ALIAS_TOOLTIP: Create a type alias using modern C++ syntax
C_FORWARD_DECL_TOOLTIP: Forward declare a function so the compiler knows it exists (define it later)
U_WHILE_TOOLTIP: Repeat while the condition is true
U_FUNC_CALL_EXPR_TOOLTIP: Call a function and get the return value
style.apcs.desc: Uses cout/cin, 4-space indent, for APCS exam
style.competitive.desc: Uses printf/scanf, 4-space indent, for competitive programming
style.google.desc: Uses cout/cin, 2-space indent, Google coding standards
SYNC_HINT_BLOCKS_CHANGED: Blocks changed, click to sync code
SYNC_HINT_CODE_CHANGED: Code changed, click to sync blocks
MODE_ADVANCED_DESC: Show all blocks including advanced features
QA_TOOLTIP_VAR: Add a variable block to workspace
QA_TOOLTIP_PRINT: Add a print block to workspace
QA_TOOLTIP_INPUT: Add an input block to workspace
QA_TOOLTIP_IF: Add a conditional block to workspace
QA_TOOLTIP_LOOP: Add a loop block to workspace
QA_TOOLTIP_FUNC: Add a function block to workspace
ERROR_SYNC_FAILED: Conversion failed, code may have syntax errors
RUNTIME_ERR_MAX_STEPS: Maximum execution steps exceeded (possible infinite loop or deep recursion)
RUNTIME_ERR_BREAK_OUTSIDE_LOOP: 'break' can only be used inside a loop
RUNTIME_ERR_CONTINUE_OUTSIDE_LOOP: 'continue' can only be used inside a loop
RUNTIME_ERR_DUPLICATE_DECLARATION: Variable '%1' is already declared in this scope
EXEC_TIMEOUT_WARNING: Execution stopped: maximum step limit reached (possible infinite loop)
EXEC_UNSYNC_PROMPT: Blocks have changed. Sync before running?
